feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: EMAIL oAUth Authentication issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on connecting from SAP Cloud Integration to a mail account in Outlook 365 via OAuth2 with Authorization Code grant type, using the protocol SMTP for sending e-mails .

Followed this blog Cloud Integration - Connect to Microsoft 365 Mail ... - SAP Community and completed the configuration also developed Generic Iflow for sending emails .But while we are testing ending up with error as Authorization unsuccessful 

 

Please find the detailed error logs in CPI

“javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: 535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful”.   Kindly advise on how to resolve this issue and what this error actually means.

entry.title: Custom Field addition to standard integration package - Package Integration SF EC to WFS
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to add a custom field FO from Employee Central "Site" into mapping file of SAP CPI standard integration package - SuccessFactors Employee Central with Third Party Time Vendor-WorkForce Software(WFS) , Artifact - Packaged Integration SF EC to WFS, so that this information can be sent to Workforce System for business purposes.

We wanted to check if this is a functionality which can be performed in SAP CPI since the package integration says "configure-only" and not editable? Are there any KBAs around this?

Regards

Pooja Ganguly

entry.title: Has anyone done encoding in CPI with scripts(UTF-8 to any) or can it be handled by only adapters?
entry.content:
I observed that UTF-8 is the default standard for CPI and when try to convert to iso-8859-1 using script, i get a lot of issues including byte mismatch or character getting corrupted which raises me a question can the encoding be handled only at adapter level, please clarify?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-convert-utf-8-string-to-iso-8859-1-using-groovy/qaq-p/14419909

If anyone have done any conversion internally in the flow level (like UTF-8 to any format like ANSI or iso 8859-1) which is working, please explain 

If not, please clarify which adapters supports encoding (i have observed that file adapters doesn't have that option, i hope http, soap and as2 should have the support, please add if i missed any)

entry.title: Exception Subprocess for IDoc Receiver Adapter
entry.content:
Hi,

We are developing a scenario, where we have an ECC system receiving IDoc from Cloud Integration through Cloud Connector. We use a request-reply shape with IDoc receiver adapter. We have an exception subprocess wrapped around the IDoc receiver adapter. However, we observe that when the call has failed (IDoc data issue, or Cloud connector is down, for example), the flow does not get into the exception subprocess; rather it simply fails at the IDoc adapter step. Screenshot of our configuration is given below. We have tried to wrap the IDoc receiver into a separate local integration process, as per suggestion from this link, to no avail. We tried to go through the help documentation, but there is no mention of this behavior as well. Any hint on this behavior will be appreciated.

Thanks,

Sid

entry.title: GCS JWT token generation - SAP CPI neo - PKIX path building failed:
entry.content:
We have a requirement to upload files from SAP CPI (Neo environment) to Google Cloud Storage. As part of this integration, we implemented an OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer flow using a Google service account.

The integration flow has been designed as follows:

  • The service account private key is stored securely in the CPI Keystore (converted to .p12 format).
  • A Groovy script retrieves the private key from the keystore and generates a signed JWT.
  • The generated JWT is then used in an HTTP adapter call to obtain an access token from Google’s OAuth token endpoint.
  • The access token is intended to be used in a subsequent HTTP call to upload files to Google Cloud Storage.

However, we are encountering an error when invoking the OAuth token endpoint:

https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token

The error message observed is:

java.net.ConnectException: PKIX path building failed: sun.security.provider.certpath.SunCertPathBuilderException: unable to find valid certification path to requested target

We have already imported the relevant Google/DigiCert certificates (including GTS Root R1 and WR2, as well as DigiCert Global Root certificates) into the CPI Neo keystore. Despite this, the PKIX error persists during the HTTPS call.

Could you please advise what might be missing or incorrectly configured in this scenario? Additionally, are there any known limitations or special considerations in SAP CPI Neo when establishing HTTPS connections to Google OAuth endpoints (oauth2.googleapis.com)?

Your guidance on how to resolve this issue, or confirmation of any platform-related constraints, would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: BTP Destination creation error
entry.content:
we are trying to create a new destination in our BTP subaccount for SBI bank integration with SFTP server.

But creation is throwing error.

entry.title: Connecting to a database with Cloud Connector changes Host and port when connecting
entry.content:
I am trying to connect to a non-sap database on premise using the Cloud connector, initial error was password was to be reset- this indicates i was able to hit the database. I had the password changed and updated the iflow and database params. 

Now however when i try to test a connection i am getting an error like below but the hostname and port are incorrect...

Any thoughts on the below error, i see a similar error posted before me but with no possible solution.....

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Failed to obtain JDBC Connection; nested exception is com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1101 has failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-login response. Verify the connection properties and check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.". ClientConnectionId:6f5d542d-ff8c-4a3d-9741-3819a2fe22e3. The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGo9T1VcOR6UcHpQKuAGN-mHR5um

Ravi Iyer

entry.title: BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 error when replicating Business Partner from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 to SAP S/
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on the integration scenario 6KO - Opportunity to Order with SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and I am facing an issue with Business Partner replication from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Scenario:
SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 → SAP Cloud Integration → SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Issue:
When a Business Partner is modified in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and the replication is triggered back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Message Monitoring in S/4HANA shows the error:

BP_DATAREPL_FND / 006

What is already configured:

  • Communication System created in S/4HANA with Business System field filled with the Display ID obtained from the default communication system in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
  • Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0008 active with the correct Communication System assigned
  • Additional Properties: Integration with C4C enabled = X and No Multiple Address Handling = X
  • Inbound Service active: Business Partner - Replicate from Client to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SOAP)
  • Communication User linked to the Communication System and Communication Arrangement
  • iFlow Replicate Business Partner to SAP S4HANA Cloud deployed and running in SAP Cloud Integration

Question:
What could be causing the error BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 with all this configuration in place? Is there any additional configuration needed specifically for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to authorize the inbound replication coming from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?

Any help or relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP CPI OData V4 POST – Invalid CSRF Token Error for WarehouseTask Confirm API (EWM A2X)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working with the standard SAP EWM API – Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), specifically using the WarehouseTask entity. This is an OData V4 API.

🔹 Scenario

I am trying to perform a POST operation to confirm a Warehouse Task using the below endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask/EU20/1234563436/0/SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskHndlgUnit

Where:

  • EU20 → EWM Warehouse
  • 1234563436 → Warehouse Task
  • 0 → Warehouse Task Item

🔹 Approach Followed

  1. First, I executed a GET call on the same API to:

    • Fetch the CSRF token
    • Retrieve the corresponding ETag for the specific combination (Warehouse, WarehouseTask, WarehouseTaskItem)in the body.

  2. For the POST request:

    • I used the same CSRF token in the header
    • Set the header:
      If-Match = <ETag (from GET response)>

🔹 Issue Faced

While triggering the POST request, I am receiving the following error:

Invalid CSRF token

🔹 Observed Behavior

  • In the CPI trace logs (receiver side), I can see that the CSRF token is being passed in the request header.
  • However, after the failure:
    • In the request headers attachment, the CSRF token appears as "fetch" instead of the actual token value.

This is creating a confusing situation, as the token seems to be passed but still results in an "Invalid CSRF token" error.

🔹 API Reference

For reference, I am using the API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub:
Warehouse Task Confirm API

🔹 Ask

Has anyone encountered a similar issue while working with this API or OData V4 CSRF handling in CPI?

Any guidance on:

  • Correct CSRF handling approach, or
  • Possible reasons why the token is getting replaced with "fetch"
    would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Define bank transfer tile App id: F3759 - Custom Field are not available in Adapt UI
entry.content: Custom field was created on "Custom Field app " using Business Context " Payment Request " and "Bank Account Master Data ", but when we are trying to add custom field on "Define bank transfer tile App id: F3759" using Adaption UI , new custom field is not available to add on app

entry.title: Solution for Attachment Replication from SAP Business Suite to sales cloud V2?
entry.content:
The standard Replicate Sales Order and Sales Quote from SAP S4HANA iFlow only covers header, products, prices, and parties "no attachments".

Is there a standard SAP iFlow/content package for replicating S/4 sales order/quote attachments to Sales Cloud V2 (similar to the old C4C AttachmentFolderReplicationRequest_Out scenario)? Or is a custom post-exit using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV → V2 AttachmentFolder the only way? or there is any other way to handle this integration

Has anyone built this in a live project?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Issue with oauth authentication while calling Integration flow
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have created one Oauth clinent and also added the role ESBMesseging.send role to it, i can fetch the access token but not able to call the endpoint iflow of https in CPI and getting 403 error

entry.title: API is still valid ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We were using the API to extract sender host type and Receiver host type .Just wanted to know if this API "{cpi_url}/api/v1/IntegrationConnections?$expand=IntegrationFlows&$filter=ResolvedConnection eq true&$inlinecount=allpages  " been retired ?  If so, Any new API been released or any API available which provide the same details.

Below is the blog from where we picked the API .

SAP Cloud Platform Integration - Connection count ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Not able to create story report
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

When trying to create story report in SF QAS instance, it is giving a blank page and giving message - "Full Screen mode toggled as we detected you are using a low resolution. More information about our minimum resolution requirements can be found at help.sap.com " 

Kindly find the attached screenshot with the error.

entry.title: SAP BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry migration using Postman collection
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I am performing SAP BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry Migration using Postman Collection provided by SAP.
I am following the steps mentioned in the following SAP Help document :  'https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration-migration-guide/migrating-cloud-integration-from-neo-environment-to-multi-cloud-foundation'.
The collection 'Connect to Tenants' executed successfully. The collection 'Custom Content' executed successfully. I am able to migrate Package successfully.

Regarding uploading 'sap_cloudintegrationcertificate' from both environments, I have followed instructions as provided in SAP Help document

When executing the collection 'CPI MIG090 Security Artifacts' got the following exception for all the requests in the collection - 
'Exception: com.sap.it.cm.transport.exception.TransportManagementBackendException: No DNS name found in the system property or user provided certificate'.

( For 'sap_cloudintegrationcertificate' in Neo environment there is 'Subject Alternative Name' which contains 'DNS' value where as it is missing in Cloud Foundry environment. )

Could anyone please provide guidance on the issue?

Thank you.

entry.title: Custom Domain on Cloud Foundry subaccount to call Integration Suite endpoint on Neo subaccount
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

We are implementing FatturaPA (Italy's tax authority eDocument requirement) for a customer running Integration Suite on a Neo subaccount. Customers must register they web service endpoint under a domain thy own (see https://me.sap.com/notes/2583309 and https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/fatturapa-edocument-italy-signing-custom-domain/qaq-p/731320).

We have performed the set up in customers running both integration suite and custom domain on Neo or with both running in cloud foundry but SAP refuses to sell customer domain on Neo because it is on sunset.

Customer is migrating from Neo to Cloud Foundry as part of a large scale revamp project starting in January 2027 so migrating Integration Suite to Cloud Foundry now is out of question but FatturaPA mus be set up by October.

Can a custom domain running in a Cloud Foundry subaccount be used to call an Integration Suite endpoint running in Neo on a different subaccount?

If yes, can you kindly let us know how or point the relevant documentation? We have already tried Routes and Sharing Reserved Domains with no luck.

If no, is there any official SAP source, such a SAP Note that says so explicitly?

Thanks in advance.

Best regards,

Jose

entry.title: Electronic Invoicing France
entry.content:
Good morning,

we are implementing incoming electronic invoicing for France and we would need a sample XML file related to a French electronic invoice.

Unfortunately, we have not been able to find an example either online or within the Build Work Zone dedicated to France.

Could you please provide us with a sample XML file to be used as a reference for our implementation activities?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - MCP Capability - API Management - When available?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'd like to start using the MCP capability of integration suite (see the video: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/agentic-ai.html ), but I wonder when it will be available on our Standard Integration Suite tenants. The A2A will take some time so the MCP is the best guess, I guess...

Any (official) idea?

All the best

Laurens

entry.title: GitHub Integration is not available for tenants
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I understood that SAP released GitHub Integration for Integration suite. I am trying to see the GitHub related options in my tenants but cant find. 

I have admin and developer role in all tenants but I am not able to see it. 

Do we have any prerequisites to enable GitHub Inytegration?

entry.title: unable to deploy the Value mapping Iflow
entry.content:
In QA environment, we are unable to deploy the Value mapping Iflow 
'Value mappings for the employee data integration' deployment failed.
Error Details

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][ArtifactDeploymentFailed] : All attempts of deployment of artifact failed. Error details: Optional[Caused By : Activator start error in bundle SAP_SuccessFactors_Employee_Central_to_WorkForce_Software_Suite_Value_Mappings.DR [1051]. | Caused By : This Value Mapping contains a group () that has an Agency Identifier pair [WFS, WFS Status Ranking] : [EC, EC Employment Status], which conflicts with group
